markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define when cloud models may assist and how their outputs remain attributable, reviewable, and non-authoritative until promoted. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: CloudFallbackRules leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CloudFallbackRules is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: MT-221 makes direct swarm chat local-only and routes cloud fallback through a receipt gate: the backend records a bound local/system fallback-basis EventLedger event before cloud spawn, refuses loose/replayed fallback basis rows, generates cloud output without live capture, persists a non-authoritative pending-review cloud-assistance receipt in PostgreSQL, and only then returns/captures cloud output. The UI requires real receipt context before enabling cloud fallback; control-room/app chat deliberately pass null rather than synthesizing claim IDs.

commands.result: PASS; app lib and handshake_core compiled with existing unrelated warnings
PASS; 3 files / 40 tests passed; existing React act warnings remain in SwarmOperatorSurface tests
PASS; 10/10 chat-generate cloud fallback/local-only receipt-gate tests passed

commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests cloud_assistance -- --nocapture
commands.result: PASS; 3/3 PostgreSQL/EventLedger cloud-assistance tests passed, including loose/replayed fallback-basis rejection
PASS; no production fake receipt/claim IDs found; only the typed IPC fallbackBasisEventId field matched

evidence.summary: Frontend boundary reviewer found that OperatorChat required receipt context but app/control-room producers were not carrying it and tests were stale. Hardened by making receiptContext required in the cloud-escalation contract, passing explicit null from producers that do not own state-recovery claim context, keeping cloud fallback disabled without real context, and updating tests to prove no fake request is sent.

Rust/backend reviewer found no scoped backend or migration blocker after hardening. Focused tests proved generic chat rejects cloud sessions without the receipt gate, cloud escalation without receipt context does not spawn or capture cloud output, and cloud assistance rejects loose/replayed fallback basis.

Frontend re-review confirmed receiptContext is required, SwarmControlRoom explicitly returns null instead of fake claim IDs, successful lower-level cloud fallback requires a real context object, TypeScript IPC fields match Rust camelCase serde, and no production fake fallback/receipt IDs remain under app/src.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
